name: Puchero
recipeIngredient: 1/4 lb Boneless lamb, cubed into 2-inch pieces
1/4 lb Boneless beef, cubed into 2-inch pieces
3 lbs Chicken, cut into serving pieces
2 White potatoes, boiled and peeled, sliced into thick slices
3 Bananas, peeled and sliced into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
Puchero is to Mexican cooking what Pot-au-Feu is to French. The difference lies in Puchero's imaginative combination of vegetables and fruits. Since it is even more delicious the second day, this recipe will make an ample amount to serve 8 for dinner, with some left over for lunch the following day.
1. Place the chick peas in a kettle and cover with broth. Soak overnight.
2. Cut the lamb and beef into 2-inch cubes. Peel and slice the onion and garlic. Combine the drained chick peas, all the meats, veal knuckle, onion, garlic, and salt in a large soup kettle. Cover with 2 quarts of cold water. Bring to a full boil. Skim off the froth.
3. Lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes.
4. Peel and slice the turnips and carrot. Peel and dice the sweet potatoes. Slice the zucchini. Peel and dice the onions. Cut the corn from the cob. Add the cabbage, turnips, carrot, zucchini, sweet potatoes, and corn to the kettle. Cover and simmer about 20 minutes or until meats and vegetables are tender.
5. Peel, core, and slice the pears and peaches. Put in a small pan with a little water and poach for 10 minutes. Do not overcook. Drain the fruit.
6. Adjust the seasonings. Serve each bowl of soup with the juice from one lime wedge and a tablespoon of guacamole.
